parameterGroups.label: Sweep Range
parameterGroups.rationale: Frequency1 and Frequency2 define the lower and upper bounds of the phaser sweep

parameters.name: Frequency 1
parameters.description: Lower frequency bound of the phaser sweep range in Hz

parameters.name: Frequency 2
parameters.description: Upper frequency bound of the phaser sweep range in Hz

parameterGroups.label: Effect
parameterGroups.rationale: Feedback controls resonance intensity, Mix controls wet/dry balance

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.description: Amount of signal fed back through the filter chain for resonance

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.description: Wet/dry balance where 0 is fully dry and 1 is fully wet

standaloneModChains.name: Phase Modulation
standaloneModChains.note: Modulates the phaser sweep position between the two frequency bounds. No parameterIndex - standalone chain.

gaps.question: What is the processing order in the phaser? How many allpass filter stages are there? Is it a cascade of allpass filters with a feedback path around all of them?
gaps.context: Need to understand the allpass filter topology for the pseudo-code.

gaps.question: How does the Phase Modulation chain interact with Frequency1 and Frequency2? Does the modulation value interpolate between the two frequencies, or does it scale/offset them?
gaps.context: Critical for explaining how users control the sweep range and modulation depth.

gaps.question: Where is the feedback applied? Is it fed back around the entire allpass chain or per-stage?
gaps.context: Feedback topology determines the resonance character.

gaps.question: How is the Mix parameter applied? Simple linear crossfade between dry and wet, or something else?
gaps.context: Need to document the dry/wet mixing behaviour accurately.

gaps.question: Does PhaseFX have an internal LFO for sweep, or does it rely entirely on the Phase Modulation chain? If there is no internal LFO, the modulation chain is the sole source of sweep movement.
gaps.context: Users need to know if the phaser sweeps on its own or requires external modulation.

gaps.question: Is the allpass processing per-sample or per-block? How many allpass stages are used?

notes: PhaseFX is categorised as 'delay' because phasers use allpass delay filters. The Phase Modulation chain is standalone (no parameterIndex). Known scriptnode equivalent: fx.phase_delay.
